source_text: In your place, I would speak with the director.

reference_translations.text: Al posto tuo, parlerei con il direttore.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. Subject pronoun dropped, the natural default.

reference_translations.text: Al posto tuo, parlarei con il direttore.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: keeps the -are a instead of shifting to e; parlerei is correct.

explanation: Parlare is a regular -are verb, so its conditional shifts the theme vowel a to e (the future stem parler-) and adds -ei: parlerei. 'Al posto tuo' (in your place) makes it a hypothetical, hence the conditional.

source_text: If we could, we would return home immediately.

reference_translations.text: Se potessimo, torneremmo subito a casa.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. torneremmo with double m.

reference_translations.text: Se potessimo, torneremo subito a casa.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: single-m torneremo is the future; the conditional needs the double m (torneremmo).

explanation: Tornare gives the conditional stem torner-, and the noi ending is the double-m -emmo: torneremmo. The single-m torneremo would be the future. The if-clause 'se potessimo' (if we could) calls for the conditional.

source_text: I would gladly have a tea, thank you.

reference_translations.text: Prenderei volentieri un tè, grazie.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. prendere = to have (a drink) here.

reference_translations.text: Prendrei volentieri un tè, grazie.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: prendrei cuts the stem as if prendere syncopated; the regular -ere stem is prender- (prenderei).

explanation: Prendere is a regular -ere verb: the conditional drops only the final -e to give prender-, plus the io ending -ei: prenderei. Accepting an offer politely is a classic conditional use.

source_text: With a little calm, I would understand the lesson better.

reference_translations.text: Con un po' di calma, capirei meglio la lezione.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. No -isc- in the conditional.

reference_translations.text: Con un po' di calma, capiscirei meglio la lezione.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: the present's -isc- (capisco) has no place in the conditional; the stem is capir- (capirei).

explanation: Capire takes -isc- in the present (capisco) but never in the conditional: the stem is simply capir-, plus -ei, giving capirei.

source_text: In your place, I would look for another job.

reference_translations.text: Al posto tuo, cercherei un altro lavoro.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. h inserted to keep the hard c.

reference_translations.text: Al posto tuo, cercerei un altro lavoro.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: without the h the c softens; -care verbs insert h (cercherei).

explanation: Cercare ends in -care, so the conditional inserts an h to keep the hard /k/: the stem is cercher- (as in cercherò), plus -ei: cercherei.

source_text: With more free time, we would travel much more.

reference_translations.text: Con più tempo libero, viaggeremmo molto di più.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. redundant i dropped, double-m noi.

reference_translations.text: Con più tempo libero, viaggieremmo molto di più.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: -giare drops the redundant i; the stem is viagger- (viaggeremmo).

explanation: Viaggiare ends in -giare, so the redundant i drops: the stem is viagger-, and the noi ending is the double-m -emmo, giving viaggeremmo.

source_text: With more free time, I would have the desire to study.

reference_translations.text: Con più tempo libero, avrei voglia di studiare.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. avrei = syncopated avere.

reference_translations.text: Con più tempo libero, averei voglia di studiare.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: keeps the theme vowel; avere syncopates to avr- (avrei).

explanation: Avere syncopates in the conditional: the theme vowel drops to give avr- (as in avrò), plus -ei: avrei. 'Avere voglia di' means to feel like / want to.

source_text: Excuse me, could you help me for a moment?

reference_translations.text: Scusi, potrebbe aiutarmi un momento?
reference_translations.notes: Formal address (Lei). potrebbe = syncopated potere, 3sg.

reference_translations.text: Scusa, potresti aiutarmi un momento?
reference_translations.notes: Informal address (tu). potresti, equally valid for an informal 'you'.

reference_translations.text: Scusi, poterebbe aiutarmi un momento?
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: keeps the theme vowel; potere syncopates to potr- (potrebbe).

explanation: Potere syncopates to potr- (as in potrò). A polite request to 'you' is either the formal potrebbe (Lei) or the informal potresti (tu); both are accepted. Keeping the vowel (poterebbe) is the slip.

source_text: You should really rest a little.

reference_translations.text: Dovresti davvero riposare un po'.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. dovresti = syncopated dovere, advice use.

reference_translations.text: Doveresti davvero riposare un po'.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: keeps the theme vowel; dovere syncopates to dovr- (dovresti).

explanation: Dovere syncopates to dovr- (as in dovrò), and the tu ending -esti gives dovresti. The conditional of dovere ('you should') is the everyday way to give advice.

source_text: I would like a coffee, please.

reference_translations.text: Vorrei un caffè, per favore.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. vorrei = double-r volere, the canonical polite request.

reference_translations.text: Vorei un caffè, per favore.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: single r; volere assimilates to the double-r vorr- (vorrei).

explanation: Vorrei ('I would like') is the most frequent conditional in everyday Italian. Volere drops its vowel and assimilates to the double-r stem vorr- (as in vorrò), plus -ei: vorrei. A single r (vorei) is the signature miss.

source_text: We would gladly come to the party, but we can't.

reference_translations.text: Verremmo volentieri alla festa, ma non possiamo.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. verremmo carries both the double r and the double m.

reference_translations.text: Veremmo volentieri alla festa, ma non possiamo.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: single r loses the assimilation; venire gives verr- (verremmo).

reference_translations.text: Verremo volentieri alla festa, ma non possiamo.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: single-m verremo is the future; the conditional noi needs the double m.

explanation: Venire takes the assimilated double-r stem verr- (as in verrò), and the noi ending is the double-m -emmo: verremmo. Two doublings have to be right; the single-r veremmo and the single-m future verremo are the two misses.

source_text: In your place, I would stay home tonight.

reference_translations.text: Al posto tuo, rimarrei a casa stasera.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. rimarrei = double-r rimanere.

reference_translations.text: Al posto tuo, rimarei a casa stasera.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: single r; rimanere assimilates to rimarr- (rimarrei).

explanation: Rimanere takes the double-r stem rimarr- (as in rimarrò), plus -ei: rimarrei. A single r (rimarei) loses the assimilation.

source_text: In your place, I would be more careful.

reference_translations.text: Al posto tuo, sarei più prudente.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. sarei = suppletive essere.

reference_translations.text: Al posto tuo, esserei più prudente.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: built from the infinitive; essere uses the suppletive stem sar- (sarei).

explanation: Essere uses the suppletive stem sar- (as in sarò), unrelated to its infinitive, plus -ei: sarei. Building from the infinitive (esserei) is the slip.

source_text: With more time, we would be happy to help.

reference_translations.text: Con più tempo, saremmo felici di aiutare.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. saremmo with double m.

reference_translations.text: Con più tempo, saremo felici di aiutare.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: single-m saremo is the future; the conditional noi needs the double m (saremmo).

explanation: Essere uses the suppletive stem sar-, and the noi ending is the double-m -emmo: saremmo. The single-m saremo would be the future.

source_text: In your place, I would not do it.

reference_translations.text: Al posto tuo, non lo farei.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. farei keeps -ar-.

reference_translations.text: Al posto tuo, non lo ferei.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: over-applies the a-to-e shift; fare keeps -ar- (farei).

explanation: Fare is one of the three -are verbs (with dare and stare) that keep the -ar- against the usual a-to-e shift: the stem is far- (as in farò), plus -ei, giving farei. Shifting the vowel (ferei) is the trap.

source_text: She would give you a hand, if she could.

reference_translations.text: Ti darebbe una mano, se potesse.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. darebbe keeps -ar-.

reference_translations.text: Ti derebbe una mano, se potesse.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: over-applies the shift; dare keeps -ar- (darebbe).

explanation: Dare keeps the -ar- like fare and stare: the stem is dar- (as in darò), and the 3sg ending is the double-b -ebbe, giving darebbe. 'Dare una mano' means to give a hand / to help.

source_text: Maria said she would come to the party.

reference_translations.text: Maria ha detto che sarebbe venuta alla festa.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. Reported future-in-the-past = conditional perfect; venire takes essere, participle agrees (venuta).

reference_translations.text: Maria ha detto che avrebbe venuto alla festa.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: venire takes essere, not avere; and the participle must agree (sarebbe venuta).

reference_translations.text: Maria ha detto che sarebbe venuto alla festa.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: participle must agree with feminine Maria (venuta, not venuto).

explanation: English 'she said she would come' maps onto the conditional perfect in Italian: the conditional of the auxiliary plus the participle. Venire takes essere (sarebbe), and the participle agrees with feminine Maria: sarebbe venuta.

source_text: He had promised he would give us a hand.

reference_translations.text: Aveva promesso che ci avrebbe dato una mano.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. avere auxiliary; 'ci' is indirect, so participle invariable (dato).

reference_translations.text: Aveva promesso che ci sarebbe dato una mano.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: dare takes avere, not essere (avrebbe dato).

reference_translations.text: Aveva promesso che ci avrebbe data una mano.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: 'ci' is an indirect object, not a preceding DOP, so no agreement (dato).

explanation: Another reported future-in-the-past, so the conditional perfect. Dare takes avere (avrebbe), and with an indirect-object 'ci' (to us) the participle stays invariable: avrebbe dato.

source_text: With the fast train, we would have arrived much earlier.

reference_translations.text: Con il treno veloce, saremmo arrivati molto prima.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. essere auxiliary (saremmo, double-m), participle agrees with plural noi (arrivati).

reference_translations.text: Con il treno veloce, saremo arrivati molto prima.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: single-m saremo is the future auxiliary; the conditional perfect needs saremmo.

reference_translations.text: Con il treno veloce, saremmo arrivato molto prima.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: participle must agree with plural noi (arrivati, not arrivato).

explanation: A past counterfactual ('we would have arrived'), so the conditional perfect. Arrivare takes essere, whose conditional 1pl is the double-m saremmo, and the participle agrees with plural noi: saremmo arrivati.

source_text: I would like an espresso, please.

reference_translations.text: Vorrei un espresso, per favore.
reference_translations.notes: Standard polite request; vorrei softens the blunt voglio.

reference_translations.text: Voglio un espresso, per favore.
reference_translations.notes: Understandable but blunt; the polite register wants the conditional vorrei.

explanation: The softening conditional: 'vorrei' (I would like) is the courteous form of 'voglio' (I want). This is the conditional's most frequent everyday job.

source_text: Could you tell me what time it is?

reference_translations.text: Potresti dirmi che ore sono?
reference_translations.notes: Informal 'you'; potresti softens puoi.

reference_translations.text: Potrebbe dirmi che ore sono?
reference_translations.notes: Formal 'you' (Lei); equally valid for a stranger.

reference_translations.text: Puoi dirmi che ore sono?
reference_translations.notes: Direct present; the courteous version uses the conditional.

explanation: 'Could you' maps onto the conditional potresti (informal) or potrebbe (formal), softening the direct 'puoi'. Both address-forms are accepted.

source_text: Would you mind opening the window?

reference_translations.text: Ti dispiacerebbe aprire la finestra?
reference_translations.notes: The deferential idiom; only works in the conditional.

reference_translations.text: Ti dispiace aprire la finestra?
reference_translations.notes: Present is more direct; the very polite version is the conditional.

explanation: 'Would you mind' is the idiom 'ti dispiacerebbe' (literally 'would it displease you'), one of the most deferential ways to ask, and it lives in the conditional.

source_text: You should rest a little.

reference_translations.text: Dovresti riposare un po'.
reference_translations.notes: Advice via the conditional dovresti, softer than the flat devi.

reference_translations.text: Devi riposare un po'.
reference_translations.notes: 'You must' is an order; advice uses the conditional dovresti.

explanation: Advice is the conditional's territory: 'dovresti' (you should) turns the blunt 'devi' (you must) into a suggestion the listener can weigh.

source_text: Vorrei un tè, per favore.

reference_translations.text: I would like a tea, please.
reference_translations.notes: vorrei = the polite 'I would like'.

reference_translations.text: I'd like a tea, please.
reference_translations.notes: Contracted, equally natural.

explanation: Recognising the softening conditional: 'vorrei' is 'I would like', the courteous form, not the blunt 'I want' (voglio).

source_text: Le dispiacerebbe chiudere la porta?

reference_translations.text: Would you mind closing the door?
reference_translations.notes: Formal Lei; the deferential dispiacerebbe idiom.

reference_translations.text: Would you mind shutting the door?
reference_translations.notes: Equally natural.

explanation: 'Le dispiacerebbe' is the very polite formal request ('would you mind'), built on the conditional of dispiacere. The English keeps 'would'.

source_text: Sapresti consigliarmi un buon ristorante?

reference_translations.text: Could you recommend a good restaurant to me?
reference_translations.notes: Polite request for help; sapresti softens sai.

reference_translations.text: Would you be able to recommend a good restaurant?
reference_translations.notes: Equally natural rendering of sapresti.

translation.reveal: sapresti - could you / would you know

explanation: 'Sapresti' (would you know how to / could you) is the courteous way to ask for help, the conditional softening the plain 'sai'.

source_text: He said he would call back later.

reference_translations.text: Ha detto che avrebbe richiamato più tardi.
reference_translations.notes: Reported future-in-the-past = compound conditional avrebbe richiamato.

reference_translations.text: Ha detto che richiamerà più tardi.
reference_translations.notes: Simple future is wrong after a past reporting verb; use the compound conditional.

reference_translations.text: Ha detto che richiamerebbe più tardi.
reference_translations.notes: Simple conditional is wrong here; Italian uses the compound (avrebbe richiamato).

explanation: Where English says 'he said he would call', Italian uses the compound conditional avrebbe richiamato, not the simple future or simple conditional. Richiamare takes avere.

source_text: Maria promised she would arrive on time.

reference_translations.text: Maria ha promesso che sarebbe arrivata in orario.
reference_translations.notes: essere auxiliary; participle agrees with feminine Maria (arrivata).

reference_translations.text: Maria promise che sarebbe arrivata in orario.
reference_translations.notes: 'promise' = 3sg passato remoto of promettere; equally valid reporting verb.

reference_translations.text: Maria promise che sarebbe arrivato in orario.
reference_translations.notes: Participle must agree with feminine Maria: arrivata.

reference_translations.text: Maria promise che arriverebbe in orario.
reference_translations.notes: Simple conditional is wrong; reported future takes the compound (sarebbe arrivata).

explanation: Reported future-in-the-past with essere: 'sarebbe arrivata', the compound conditional agreeing with the feminine subject. English keeps 'would arrive'.

source_text: They assured us they would finish by Friday.

reference_translations.text: Ci assicurarono che avrebbero finito entro venerdì.
reference_translations.notes: Compound conditional avrebbero finito; avere, invariable participle.

reference_translations.text: Ci assicurarono che finiranno entro venerdì.
reference_translations.notes: Simple future is wrong after a past reporting verb.

reference_translations.text: Ci assicurarono che sarebbero finito entro venerdì.
reference_translations.notes: Finire takes avere, not essere; and no agreement (avrebbero finito).

explanation: Plural reported future-in-the-past: 'avrebbero finito'. Finire takes avere, so the participle is invariable and the auxiliary is avrebbero.

source_text: Disse che sarebbe tornato il giorno dopo.

reference_translations.text: He said he would come back the next day.
reference_translations.notes: Compound conditional -> English 'would come back'.

reference_translations.text: He said that he would return the following day.
reference_translations.notes: Equally natural.

explanation: The compound conditional 'sarebbe tornato' reports a future seen from the past; English renders it with 'would'. Recognising this construction is the skill.

source_text: Aveva promesso che ci avrebbe aiutato.

reference_translations.text: He had promised he would help us.
reference_translations.notes: Compound conditional -> 'would help'.

reference_translations.text: He had promised that he would help us.
reference_translations.notes: Equally natural.

explanation: 'Avrebbe aiutato' is the compound conditional for a promised future in the past; English keeps 'would help'.

source_text: Sapevo che sarebbe stata una buona idea.

reference_translations.text: I knew it would be a good idea.
reference_translations.notes: Compound conditional of essere -> 'would be'.

reference_translations.text: I knew that it would be a good idea.
reference_translations.notes: Equally natural.

explanation: Even essere uses the compound conditional here: 'sarebbe stata' reports a future-in-the-past, rendered 'would be' in English. The participle agrees (stata, with the feminine idea).

source_text: If I had time, I would help you.

reference_translations.text: Se avessi tempo, ti aiuterei.
reference_translations.notes: Congiuntivo imperfetto protasis + present conditional apodosis.

reference_translations.text: Se avrei tempo, ti aiuterei.
reference_translations.notes: The protasis takes the congiuntivo (avessi), never the conditional.

reference_translations.text: Se avessi tempo, ti aiuto.
reference_translations.notes: The apodosis must be conditional (aiuterei), not present.

explanation: The standard unreal period: 'se' + congiuntivo imperfetto in the if-clause (se avessi), conditional in the main clause (aiuterei). The apodosis is where the conditional lives.

source_text: If we studied more, we would pass the exam.

reference_translations.text: Se studiassimo di più, supereremmo l'esame.
reference_translations.notes: Apodosis conditional supereremmo (double-m).

reference_translations.text: Se studiassimo di più, supereremo l'esame.
reference_translations.notes: Single-m supereremo is the future; the apodosis needs the conditional supereremmo.

reference_translations.text: Se studiassimo di più, superavamo l'esame.
reference_translations.notes: Colloquial imperfect; the standard written apodosis is the conditional.

explanation: Unreal period: the apodosis takes the conditional supereremmo. This item also evidences the standard-over-colloquial choice (not the imperfect superavamo), so it credits the tense-selection bucket too.

source_text: If you had told me, I would have come.

reference_translations.text: Se me l'avessi detto, sarei venuto.
reference_translations.notes: Past-unreal: congiuntivo trapassato + compound conditional.

reference_translations.text: Se me lo avessi detto, sarei venuta.
reference_translations.notes: Feminine speaker: sarei venuta.

reference_translations.text: Se me l'avessi detto, verrei.
reference_translations.notes: Past-unreal apodosis needs the compound conditional (sarei venuto), not the simple.

explanation: A past-unreal period: the if-clause is congiuntivo trapassato (avessi detto) and the apodosis is the compound conditional (sarei venuto/a). The simple conditional verrei would misplace it in the present.

source_text: Se potessi, verrei con voi.

reference_translations.text: If I could, I would come with you.
reference_translations.notes: Conditional apodosis -> English 'would come'.

reference_translations.text: If I were able to, I would come with you.
reference_translations.notes: Equally natural.

explanation: Recognising the apodosis: 'verrei' is the conditional main clause of an unreal period, rendered 'I would come'.

source_text: Se avessi saputo, non sarei venuto.

reference_translations.text: If I had known, I would not have come.
reference_translations.notes: Past-unreal: compound conditional -> 'would not have come'.

reference_translations.text: Had I known, I wouldn't have come.
reference_translations.notes: Inversion, equally natural.

translation.reveal: sarei venuto - I would have come

explanation: The compound conditional 'sarei venuto' in the apodosis of a past-unreal period renders English 'would have come'. The past-unreal calls for the compound, not the simple, conditional.

source_text: Al posto tuo, non lo farei.

reference_translations.text: In your place, I wouldn't do it.
reference_translations.notes: 'Al posto tuo' is an implicit hypothetical; conditional apodosis.

reference_translations.text: If I were you, I wouldn't do it.
reference_translations.notes: Equally natural rendering of the implicit condition.

explanation: 'Al posto tuo' (in your place / if I were you) is an implicit protasis, so 'farei' is a conditional apodosis: 'I would (not) do it'.
